title: SearchEvalQuery
description: Label taxonomy for the web-search eval query set. One JSON object per query (stored as JSONL). All slice analysis depends on these labels; new labels must stay backward compatible.

qid.description: Unique ID; suggested format q-{vertical}-{seq}, e.g. q-tech-0042

query.description: The raw query text, kept in its original form without cleaning

intent.description: navigational=find a specific site/page; informational=find facts/knowledge; transactional=find an executable action (download/purchase/API); multi_hop_research=requires synthesis across multiple sources

difficulty.description: head=popular high-frequency; torso=mid-frequency; tail=long-tail / rare entities / deep pages

freshness.description: evergreen=answer stays stable long-term; recent=information from the last few weeks to months; realtime=minute/hour granularity (stock prices, scores, breaking news)

vertical.description: Vertical. medical_legal is a separate category because it has the highest authority requirements and different scoring criteria

language.description: BCP-47, e.g. zh-CN / en / ja. For cross-language queries (a Chinese query about English content) use zh-CN>en

form.description: keyword=human keyword style; natural_question=natural-language long question; agent_generated=real agent queries sampled from API traffic logs (their distribution differs significantly from humans'; must form a separate stratum)

source.description: Origin. traffic_log samples must be anonymized; public_benchmark must record provenance in meta.benchmark

gold.description: Optional. Required for objective anchor queries; may be empty for subjective pairwise queries

gold_urls.description: List of canonical URLs (navigational type). Matching rules live in anchor_scorer: three levels supported — exact / prefix / domain

meta.description: Free-form extension area: benchmark provenance, sampling batch, annotator, notes
